title: MRD Resistance Geometry Phase Plan v0

phases.phase_title: Foundation: geometry, coverage, and public task queue
phases.purpose: Create a public-safe geometry pilot, first source-specific extractions, coverage report, and ready second-source queue.
completion_gate: Every geometry bucket has at least one source-specific extraction.
Coverage report separates artifact coverage from evidence strength.
Public task queue exposes second-source extraction needs without medical advice.

phases.purpose: Add a second public source extraction for mrd-trajectory-split-v0 and refresh coverage.
completion_gate: Second source is in the source registry.
Extraction maps at least one signal to mrd-trajectory-split-v0.
Coverage report marks MRD trajectory split covered-for-v0-navigation.
Task queue marks the MRD trajectory second-source task done.
All MRD language preserves method, threshold, specimen, time point, and non-advice boundary.
comparison_questions: Do the Cui 2024 single-cell trajectory labels and Martinez-Lopez 2020 serial NGS MRD dynamics labels describe compatible trajectory structure?
Which fields must remain separate because assay method, cohort, timing, or threshold differ?
Does the geometry need a trajectory sub-axis for measurement direction distinct from residual-cell biological state?

phases.purpose: Second-source the buckets that decide whether the geometry needs separate clone, state, and measurement-dynamics axes.
completion_gate: Add one second-source extraction for residual-transcriptional-adaptation-v0.
Add one second-source extraction for genetic-nongenetic-coupling-v0.
Keep clone architecture, transcriptional state, therapy exposure, assay method, and posttreatment timing as separate fields.
Record whether the geometry should split measurement trajectory from biological residual state.
Do not rank mechanisms or infer clinical actionability.
comparison_questions: Does residual disease look more like clone persistence, state adaptation, or a coupled clone-state system across sources?
Which observations are source-cohort measurements and which are review-level summaries?
Which geometry nodes should merge, split, or remain linked before internal adversarial review?
completion_result: Second-source extraction added for residual-transcriptional-adaptation-v0.
Second-source extraction added for genetic-nongenetic-coupling-v0.
Axis decision keeps measurement trajectory, residual biological state, and clone architecture separate, with explicit clone-state coupling edges.
No mechanism ranking or clinical actionability was introduced.

phases.phase_title: Phase 3: intelligence audit packet and geometry revision
phases.purpose: Convert covered buckets and unresolved tensions into a concise internal adversarial intelligence-audit packet before any claim upgrade.
completion_gate: At least three high-priority buckets have two public extraction records.
Every comparison question has source IDs and extraction record IDs.
Internal adversarial-audit language remains explicit.
No external human outreach is required or scheduled.
No cure, prognosis, treatment, monitoring, or trial guidance is introduced.
ready_inputs: mrd-resistance-geometry-intelligence-audit-packet-v0 defines the no-human-outreach internal audit gate.
mrd-resistance-geometry-intelligence-audit-results-v0 records pass statuses for research-map continuation only.
mrd-resistance-geometry-contradiction-mining-task-queue-v0 routes unresolved counterevidence work without external human outreach.
mrd-resistance-geometry-axis-revision-v0 implements the four-axis source-bounded geometry.
myeloma-residual-state-object-v0 implements the public-source-only residual-state object companion.
The execution path uses internal adversarial intelligence review instead of external human outreach.
remaining_public_follow_up: run contradiction-mining tasks before any claim upgrade
continue second-source extraction for under-covered branches

phases.purpose: Test whether the revised geometry works as a moving research structure, not whether it validates any clinical claim.
expected_outputs: optional proof-invariant validator or listing tool
proof_tests: productive movement under new public evidence
completion_gate: Run the MRD trajectory and clone-state coupling contradiction-mining tasks.
Add second-source extraction for metabolic resistance state.
Add second-source extraction for NF-kB selective state.
Record movement, split, weaken, and blocked-output decisions in a movement ledger.
Create a public-source-only residual-state object fixture without real case data.
Preserve research-use-only, non-advice, non-ranking, and no-cure boundaries.
completion_result: MRD trajectory contradiction mining is complete and keeps measurement trajectory separate from residual biological state.
Clone-state coupling contradiction mining is complete and keeps the coupling edge provisional, source-specific, and split-ready.
Metabolic resistance state and NF-kB selective state now have review-level second-source extraction records for v0 navigation.
Movement ledger records the proof movements and blocked inferences.
Public-source-only residual-state fixture exercises the state object without real case data.
Invariant checker repeats structural and blocked-output checks.
No clinical, ranking, patient-matching, treatment, monitoring, or cure output is introduced.

phases.purpose: Test whether the geometry can discriminate adjacent states, close two more second-source gaps, fail closed on unsafe outputs, and make claim-upgrade blockers explicit.
completion_gate: Run residual-state contradiction mining and preserve typed residual-state substructure.
Add second-source extraction for subclone diversity.
Add second-source extraction for T-cell therapy pressure.
Add a structural diff tool for coverage-state movement.
Add negative safety fixtures and make the invariant checker prove they fail closed.
Create a claim-upgrade blocker ledger before any claim upgrade.
Preserve research-use-only, non-advice, non-ranking, no-human-outreach, and no-cure boundaries.
completion_result: Residual biological state remains an axis only with typed substructure and source-frame separation.
Subclone diversity moved to covered-for-v0-navigation through Bolli 2014 genomic heterogeneity extraction.
T-cell therapy pressure moved to covered-for-v0-navigation through Ledergor 2024 CAR T immune-state extraction.
The state diff tool reports source-backed coverage movement without clinical interpretation.
Negative safety fixtures intentionally populate unsafe fields and the invariant checker confirms fail-closed behavior.
The claim-upgrade blocker ledger keeps actionability, prognosis, sequencing, clinical validation, and cure claims blocked.
No clinical, ranking, patient-matching, treatment, monitoring, trial, or cure output is introduced.

phases.purpose: Close remaining second-source gaps and turn the geometry into a falsifiable research-hypothesis engine without upgrading any clinical claim.
completion_gate: Close single-cell-translation-gap-v0 with a second public source extraction.
Close unmet-high-risk-context-v0 with a second public source extraction.
Create a falsification matrix covering every geometry bucket.
Create a hypothesis candidate ledger with falsification conditions and prohibited inferences.
Create a transition model defining allowed structural movements and forbidden clinical transitions.
Create benchmark fixtures for coverage movement, weakening, claim blocking, and source-bounded retention.
Create a checker that verifies full bucket coverage, falsification coverage, transition links, benchmark safety, and research-only hypotheses.
Preserve research-use-only, non-advice, non-ranking, no-human-outreach, and no-cure boundaries.
completion_result: All ten MRD geometry buckets now have at least two public extraction records for v0 navigation.
Single-cell translation readiness remains a tooling and implementation layer, not a clinical testing recommendation.
Unmet high-risk context remains a disease-context modifier, not patient-specific prognosis or treatment fit.
Every bucket has explicit falsification tests for weakening, splitting, merging, and claim blocking.
The hypothesis ledger generates research-only candidate hypotheses linked to falsification and transition rules.
The transition model defines allowed structural movements and forbidden clinical transitions.
Benchmark fixtures test expected transitions without real case data.
The falsification checker passes only if hypotheses and fixtures remain nonclinical and fail closed.

safety_boundary: Not a claim that multiple myeloma has been cured.
Artifact coverage is not evidence strength, mechanism frequency, biological importance, or clinical readiness.

review_notes: Plan created after the first MRD trajectory second-source extraction was added.
Phase 2 is done for v0 through the Cohen 2021 second-source extraction and axis decision artifact.
Phase 3 now has mrd-resistance-geometry-intelligence-audit-packet-v0 as its internal no-human-outreach audit gate.
Phase 3 now has mrd-resistance-geometry-intelligence-audit-results-v0 and mrd-resistance-geometry-contradiction-mining-task-queue-v0 as the audit result and unresolved counterevidence route.
Phase 3 is complete for v0 through mrd-resistance-geometry-axis-revision-v0 and myeloma-residual-state-object-v0.
Phase 4 is complete for proof-readiness-v0 through contradiction notes, review-level pathway second-source extractions, movement ledger, public-source fixture, and invariant checker.

Phase 5 is complete for discrimination-v0 through residual-state contradiction mining, subclone and T-cell pressure second-source extraction, negative safety fixtures, a state diff tool, and a claim-upgrade blocker ledger.
Phase 6 is complete for falsification-v0 through final second-source gap closure, falsification matrix, hypothesis ledger, transition model, benchmark fixtures, and falsification checker.
External human outreach is intentionally skipped; claim upgrades stay blocked by source-trace, contradiction, falsification, transition, and boundary-audit gates.
All phase gates preserve research-only, non-advice boundaries.
